LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER MAXINE DOOGAN'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS DENIED. AS TO THE TIMELINESS OF THE WRIT, ONE SHOULD READ BOTH THE ELECTION CODE PROVISION AND THE SAN FRANCISCO ORDINANCE AND HARMONIZE THEM, BUT IN TERMS OF THE MERITS, THE CASE FALLS WITHIN MANDICINO V. MAGGARD (1989) 210 CAL. APP. 3D 1413. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

calendar.matters:
Second Amended Notice Of Motion
Off Calendar Mar-30-2009 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Dept. 403 3/30/2009
Judge: Patrick Mahoney; Clerk: Prisiclla Agbunag; Reporter: Maria Torreanno CSR# 8600; Attorney Philip S. Horne for Petitioner. Attorney James R. Sutton and William B. Tunick for Real Party In Interest. After review of the pleadings and agrument submission the Court grants the motion for Attorney fees and costs $15,600.00 for Fees and $2,772.00 for Cost. The Sutton Law Firm to prepare order.

Amended Notice Of Motion Of Application Fo Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Mar-30-2009 Granted Per Written Order

Amended Notice Of Motion Of Application Fo Attorney Fees And Costs
Off Calendar Mar-27-2009 Continued To Mar-30-09 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 403

Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs By Real Parties In Terest Pamela Lopinto, Laurie Fields, Alicia Boccellari, Yasmin Kaderali, Aundre Herron, Andrea Bass, Kathleen
Off Calendar Feb-06-2009 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: REAL PARTY IN INTEREST MICHELA ALIOTO-PIER, PAMELA LOPINTO, LAURIE FIELDS, ALICIA BOCCELLARI, YASMIN KADERALI, AUNDRE HERRON, ANDREA BASS, KATHLEEN WATKINS' Motion for Attorneys Fees And Costs By Real Parties In Interest, Pamela Lopinto, Laurie Fields, Alicia Boccellari, Yasmin Kaderali, Aundre Herron, Andrea Bass, Kathleen Watkins. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Off Calendar. Moving Party to call Judge Mahoney's Clerk in Department 403 at 551-3741 to calendar a new hearing date before him. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR 5797

Petition For Writ Of Mandate
Off Calendar Sep-08-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Wayne Snodgrass and Mollie Lee of the Office of the City Attorney (554.4675) for respondent and opposing party
Phil Horne (577.1170) for petitioner and moving party
James Sutton and Kevin Heneghan of the Sutton Law Firm (732.7700) for real parties in interest and opposing parties

Argued and the Court ruled as follows: PETITIONER MAXINE DOOGAN'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is denied. As to the timeliness of the writ, one should read both the election code provision and the San Francisco Ordinance and harmonize them, but in terms of the merits, the case falls within Mandicino v. Maggard (1989) 210 Cal. App. 3d 1413. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
